what_it_does: Defines the Suite class, the shared runtime kernel for the Company composition system. It is meant to be called by both the UI bridge and the MCP server, operating over the same addressed store. It provides generic graph/node operations, registry discovery, RHM chat with native tool-calling, governance/inbox, UI address resolution, context retrieval, voice handling, walkthrough/review sessions, self-build wire dispatch, corpus capture/embed, and a large set of authoring helpers.
observations: The file is dominated by a single Suite class with hundreds of methods and extensive inline docstrings marked with feature codes (S1, E1, X17, G4, etc.).

It discovers many registries from sibling directories at init time: roles, projections, lifters, mark_types, generation_policies, relation_types, ai_tics, forms, minds, skills, contexts, guides, and modes.
It exposes the RHM chat path via chat() and the staged multi-part chat_parts() generator, both routing through a shared _chat_part_core and using native tool-calling.
It implements address-keyed context resolution (R2) with recency, proximity, pin, and semantic scoring over annotations, chats, events, howtos, and run-version history.

It contains a governed self-build wire: dispatch_decision binds a build-intent to an operator approve event by seq, enforces exactly-once dispatch, runs verification, design/form lint, scope-diff, git checkpointing, and surfaces results for review.
It carries a UI_REGISTRY built from hand-authored region rows plus corpus element rows loaded from design/_system/addresses.json.
It provides many operator-only surfaces: annotate, attach_chat, pin, defer/revive offers, approve_reach, checkpoint, revert_self_change, and address-view audit trails.
standouts: The R2 context-resolution engine: gathers and scores addressed context by recency, tree proximity, pin state, and semantic relevance to the operator's intent.
The RHM verb registry (RHM_VERB_SPECS) and native tool-calling integration, with mode/context-aware availability and deterministic governance routing.
The self-build wire's fail-loud verification chain: affected suites + drift + adversarial critic + form lint + scope-diff + git checkpoint + mandatory review surface.
Mode stacking/overlays: a base mode plus partial axis overrides resolved at read time.
File-discovered registries as the single source of truth for roles, projections, corpus types, and modes.
conventions: Heavy use of inline docstrings as design records, citing acceptance tests, AGENTS.md, and specific dates/decisions.
Fail-loud validation with explicit ValueError/GovernanceError rather than silent defaults.
Lazy/local imports to avoid circular dependencies with bridge.py, cognition.py, and other runtime modules.
Class constants for tunable knobs, resolved from environment into instance attributes in __init__.
Reuse-don't-parallel: many methods delegate to existing modules (scheduler, cognition, rules, corpus, vector_index) rather than reimplementing logic.
Governance routing through guard() and posture() for AUTO vs CONFIRM/SURFACE/LOCKED actions.
concerns: The file is extremely large and central, making Suite a very broad 'god class' that may be hard to test, review, and refactor.
Many features are marked as flagged, deferred, or TODO, suggesting ongoing construction and incomplete wiring.
Heavy reliance on external files and registries that are not shown in the snippet; behavior depends on their presence and freshness.
Thread-safety claims rely on in-process locks and file locking primitives whose implementation is not visible here.
Several complex paths (chat_parts staging, wire dispatch, R2 semantic ranking) have many interacting knobs and fallback behaviors that could be brittle.
notes: This is the central runtime brain of the system: a single class that tries to hold every operator-facing, agent-facing, and self-modifying operation behind governed, registry-true, fail-loud seams.
questions: What is the exact contract between Suite and bridge.py/MCP face for request routing and serialization?
How are the file-discovered registry classes structured, and what happens when a registry file is malformed at startup?
What guarantees does the store provide for append_event, set_ref, memo_get, and graph_lock across processes?
How is the embedder failure path exercised in production, and does the semantic ranking degrade cleanly?
Which methods are intended to be exposed on the MCP face versus the UI bridge versus internal use only?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is the shared brain/core operations for both UI and MCP faces over the addressed store. The actual content matches that but has grown into a much broader orchestrator covering chat, cognition, voice, walkthroughs, self-build, corpus, and authoring, so it is more of a monolithic runtime kernel than a narrow composition core.
apparent_themes: addressable UI and context as first-class concepts
deterministic routing by policy posture rather than confidence
intention_signals: The file is trying to make one long-lived Suite instance the authoritative runtime brain that discovers all capabilities from files and the corpus, grounds the conversational RHM in live system state, stages multi-part cognition, and only mutates the system through whitelisted, operator-approved, git-reversible actions.
novelty: The combination of address-keyed context resolution with decay scoring, mode-as-data with overlays, native tool-calling RHM, and a self-build wire that auto-commits each approved autonomous build as a revertible git checkpoint before marking it implemented.
contribution: A concrete, heavily annotated runtime kernel that wires graph execution, RHM conversation, governance/inbox, file-discovered registries, UI addressing, context resolution, and autonomous build dispatch into one callable substrate shared by the UI and agent faces.
summary_for_embedding: runtime/suite.py defines the Suite class, the shared runtime kernel for the Company composition system, exposing graph operations, registry discovery, RHM chat with native tool-calling, governance/inbox, address-keyed context resolution, and a governed self-build wire.
intention_for_embedding: To provide a single, long-lived, operator-gated runtime brain that both UI and MCP faces call, discovering capabilities from files and the corpus rather than hardcoding them.
